channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Scientists discover hidden quantum world inside cobalt
item.description: Advanced measurements reveal unexpected quantum complexity in cobalt, a metal previously thought fully understood.

item.title: Hidden black hole pairs may now have visible signal
item.description: Scientists propose a new method to find supermassive black hole pairs by searching for stars that flash repeatedly.

item.title: Octopuses use mirrors to find food they cannot see
item.description: Octopuses may be even smarter than previously thought. Dartmouth researchers discovered that octopuses can learn to use mirrors to locate hidden food.

item.title: Magnetic fields may be the secret behind binary star formation
item.description: Scientists have identified a surprising force that may explain how binary star systems form so quickly. New supercomputer simulations suggest that magnetic fields play a key role.
